Macros for calculating array lengths and member sizes in C.

# Purpose
This C header file defines three macros to assist with array and structure size calculations. The `ARRAY_LENGTH` macro calculates the number of elements in an array by dividing the total size of the array by the size of its first element. The `STATIC_ARRAY_LENGTH` macro computes the length of a literal array at compile time, which is useful in contexts that require constant expressions, by dividing the size of the array by the size of its element type. The `MEMBER_SIZE` macro determines the size of a specific member within a structure by using a null pointer cast to the structure type and accessing the member. These macros facilitate size calculations in C programs, enhancing code readability and maintainability.
